title: Misatos encouragement MAJOR SPOILERS
wikitext:
[]: Misato's encouragement (MAJOR SPOILERS)             Quote Post by ehh123 » Thu Jul 08, 2010 2:55 pm At the end of Evangelion 2.0, Misato is cheering Shinji on to save Rei. It was clear from Ritsuko that what Shinji was doing was potentially dangerous. Why was she encouraging Shinji anyway? Did she know that it would cause Third Impact or something as bad? If she did, why the encouragement?

[]:             Quote Post by subAtomic » Thu Jul 08, 2010 3:22 pm She was encouraging Shinji to do something for himself for once, instead of for other people. I do not believe she had any idea it would trigger Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jul 08, 2010 3:29 pm What subAtomic said. She seemed pretty surprised when Third Impact finally began.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Jul 08, 2010 8:00 pm What they said^^^. She just assumed it was one of the usual Eva 01 going berserk scenes so she decided to encourage Shinji since he was in control. She had no idea that the power Unit 01 had at that time could cause 3rd impact. If she actually knew this before,she would of been shouting at him to stop instead.

[]:             Quote Post by ehh123 » Thu Jul 08, 2010 8:47 pm Don't get me wrong. It was good for Misato to cheer on Shinji for FINALLY doing something proactive. It gives me one more reason to love her. I was just wondering why she was doing that when the scientists were telling Shinji not to go "too deep" or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Jul 08, 2010 9:00 pm ehh123 wrote: Don't get me wrong. It was good for Misato to cheer on Shinji for FINALLY doing something proactive. It gives me one more reason to love her. I was just wondering why she was doing that when the scientists were telling Shinji not to go "too deep" or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Jul 08, 2010 9:22 pm ehh123 wrote: Don't get me wrong. It was good for Misato to cheer on Shinji for FINALLY doing something proactive. It gives me one more reason to love her. I was just wondering why she was doing that when the scientists were telling Shinji not to go "too deep" or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 08, 2010 11:50 pm ^ She's not stupid, she just lacks adequate information. Also, i'm 100% sure that the following conversation took place during almost-Third Impact: Hyuga:"Well, Katsuragi-san... the world is ending... and i'm still a virgin... and..." Misato:"No."

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Fri Jul 09, 2010 8:32 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Also, i'm 100% sure that the following conversation took place during almost-Third Impact: Hyuga:"Well, Katsuragi-san... the world is ending... and i'm still a virgin... and..." Misato:"No."

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Sat Jul 10, 2010 12:22 pm As people have totally brought up already, Misato's very reckless. You see this quite well in the TV series when she's climbing into nuclear robots that are about to explode. In both versions, you see this when she's driving her shit car through a war zone to pick up Shinji. I'd say she has little care for her own safety (I don't think she likes herself very much either, hence why she slaps herself in the face in the first film). But she definitely worries more about other people than she worries about herself, and this often comes about in how she cares more about the pilots than about the big picture, whereas all the mad science freaks at Nerv are like "THEY CAN BE REPLACED MUA HA HA". So when she's cheering Shinji on, I think that's where she's coming from. In her heart she really wants the pilots to be happy (they're child soldiers with fucked up lives so they could use some extra happiness) and she's thinking of that. She runs on instict and gut reactions. Her first thought isn't going to be about the big picture. In that sense, she's more of a mother to Shinji than Yui could ever be . Hell, she's basically the mother of all the pilots. When she has that phone chat with Asuka in 2.xx, I also got that impression. Where are all the parental figures for these kids? Misato's the best thing they've got. Is she stupid? You could argue Ritsuko is "smarter" than Misato, but who is "smarter" when it comes to human emotion? Definitely Misato. Plus, her job at Nerv is to deal with tactics on the field. She deals with things as they come. "How can I kill this monster?" not "How will this change geo-politics in the next twenty years?" Thinking about the big picture is the job of other people. So this scene, to me, is classic Misato. And it makes me smile.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jul 12, 2010 12:03 pm ehh123 wrote: Why was she encouraging Shinji anyway? Did she know that it would cause Third Impact or something as bad? If she did, why the encouragement?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Mon Jul 12, 2010 4:25 pm I agree with absolutely everything the O-Girl said; great post overall. Misato's 'stupidity' is really just that she's the only one who cares about these kid's well-being.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Tue Jul 13, 2010 10:12 am Sachi wrote: What subAtomic said. She seemed pretty surprised when Third Impact finally began.

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Tue Jul 13, 2010 11:13 am Nerv isn't precisely the most open organization; Misato was living with a totally incorrect understanding of pretty much everything all the way through the original series, and arguably died without getting the story straight. No matter how smart she is, she isn't given access to the proper information to know whether or not this is a good idea, and vague statements of "it's dangerous" in the eva-verse have typically been met with increased gung-ho behavior and defeated. At this point, in Misato's mind, it's Rei dying versus some ambiguous potentially-bad thing maybe-happening. Transparency: 0 3i: 1

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 13, 2010 2:36 pm I'm thinking that the truth lies somewhere between Carla and oOoOoOo's statements. Misato is clearly the closest thing the pilots have to an advocate, but she's also just using them in her own way. Against any odds or opposition she'll back the pilots... right up to the point where their sacrifice is necessary to kill an angel. That still takes precedence, and though she'd almost certainly rationalize it as necessary in the bigger picture (and it probably would be) it's still her own need for vengeance that is the primary motivation involved.

[]:             Quote Post by ImpedingAngel » Sat Jul 17, 2010 1:16 am [s]Gogogogogogogo, Shinji-kun![/s] Here's my beef: If the power to Eva-01 was off and running on Shinji-Powerthirst Rage Flavor for the allotment of the Misato pep rally, how did he hear her? Shinji just happened to have some genuine 'God I hate this Rei snatching monster' feelings after watching her get nom'd on and when he could finally do something about it, he gave Zeruel a mild spanking and was out of batteries before the show got started. So Eva reactivated only after his rage biscuits kicked in, and every time the thing goes bonkers, they lose communications, amirite??? how'd he hear her?? Fix this?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sat Jul 17, 2010 1:20 am ImpedingAngel wrote: [s]Gogogogogogogo, Shinji-kun![/s] Here's my beef: If the power to Eva-01 was off and running on Shinji-Powerthirst Rage Flavor for the allotment of the Misato pep rally, how did he hear her? Shinji just happened to have some genuine 'God I hate this Rei snatching monster' feelings after watching her get nom'd on and when he could finally do something about it, he gave Zeruel a mild spanking and was out of batteries before the show got started. So Eva reactivated only after his rage biscuits kicked in, and every time the thing goes bonkers, they lose communications, amirite??? how'd he hear her?? Fix this?

[]:             Quote Post by insomniac » Sat Jul 17, 2010 10:23 pm Having watched the R3 DVD subs a few days ago, I can see where this question is coming from, if the Hong Kong official subs was the version you watched. In that version, the subs do give the impression that she's saying "Woo! Go Shinji!! Bring it on!" Whereas the fansubbed version I watched earlier on, made it clear that the message was "Do something for yourself for once, instead of trying to please someone else".

[]:             Quote Post by Tails_night » Sun Jul 18, 2010 6:52 pm I felt that it was mroe her encouraging to do something for himself, to stand up for what he believes in and wants regardless of what other say, and regardless of the consequences to protect those he loves. As opposed to sit back and taking it all the time, then running and hiding.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jul 19, 2010 1:49 pm ImpedingAngel wrote: [s]Gogogogogogogo, Shinji-kun![/s]
